questions.question: Select the most appropriate SYNONYM of the word “Viable.”
questions.explanation: A) Viable (adjective) – Feasible, workable, practicable, applicable, executable. (व्यवहार्य/काम करने योग्य) Synonym: Feasible (adjective) – Possible to do easily or conveniently. (संभव/व्यवहार्य) • Fearless (adjective): Lacking fear. (निडर) • Feastful (adjective): Relating to a feast; festive. (उत्सवी) • Featureless (adjective): Lacking distinctive attributes or aspects. (विशेषताहीन)

questions.question: From the alternatives given below, select the correct meaning of the idiom. To have a finger in every pie.
options: To be involved in a situation, often in a meddlesome way
To be involved in several different activities or enterprises at once, especially when maintaining some level of influence over those activities
Regardless of how a situation is analyzed, viewed, or portrayed, the truth of it remains unchanged
To have a portion of the money or profits that are being shared by everyone involved in generating them
questions.explanation: B) To have a finger in every pie (idiom) – To be involved in several different activities or enterprises at once, especially when maintaining some level of influence over those activities कई कामों में एक साथ शामिल होना • To be involved in a situation, often in a meddlesome way – किसी मामले में दखल देना • Regardless of how a situation is analyzed, viewed, or portrayed, the truth of it remains unchanged – सच्चाई नहीं बदलती • To have a portion of the money or profits that are being shared by everyone involved in generating them – लाभ में हिस्सा होना

questions.question: Select the most appropriate SYNONYM of the word “Glut.”
questions.explanation: C) Glut (noun) – Surplus, excess, surfeit, superfluity, plethora. (भरमार/अधिकता) Synonym: Surfeit (noun) – An excessive amount of something. (आधिक्य / अतिरेक) • Servile (adjective): Having or showing an excessive willingness to serve or please others. (चापलूसी पूर्ण) • Sophistry (noun): The use of clever but false arguments, especially with the intention of deceiving. (कुतर्क) • Sordid (adjective): Involving ignoble actions and motives; arousing moral distaste and contempt. (घिनौना)

questions.question: Which of the following idiom given in the alternatives has the meaning? Slightly unwell or in low spirits.
questions.explanation: D) Under the weather (idiom) – Slightly unwell or in low spirits थोड़ा अस्वस्थ या उदास महसूस करना

questions.question: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the word “Gruelling.”
questions.explanation: B) Gruelling (adjective) – Exhausting, taxing, demanding, arduous, strenuous. (भीषण / थका देने वाला) Antonym: Facile (adjective) – Appearing neat and comprehensive only by ignoring the true complexities; easy. (आसान / सरल) • Fetid (adjective): Smelling extremely unpleasant; having a heavy offensive odor. (दुर्गंधित) • Feral (adjective): In a wild state, especially after escape from captivity or domestication. (जंगली) • Feyly (adjective/adv): Giving an impression of vague unworldliness or magical mystery. (विलक्षण / अलौकिक)

questions.question: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the word “Fabricate.”
questions.explanation: D) Fabricate (verb) – Forge, counterfeit, falsify, concoct, feign. (जाली बनाना) Antonym: Authenticate (verb) – To prove or show something (such as a claim or document) to be true or genuine. (प्रमाणित करना) • Ameliorate (verb): To make something bad or unsatisfactory better. (सुधारना) • Acquiesce (verb): To accept something reluctantly but without protest. (मौन सहमति देना) • Annihilate (verb): To destroy utterly; to obliterate. (मिटा देना)

questions.question: Choose the most suitable one-word substitution for the following. A group of people who travel with an important or famous person.
questions.explanation: D) Entourage (noun) – A group of people who accompany an important or famous person साथ चलने वाला दल • Gambit (noun) – A strategic move or opening चाल / रणनीतिक शुरुआत • Cohorts (noun) – Companions or supporters साथी / सहयोगी • Caravan (noun) – A group of travelers, especially across desert यात्रियों का काफिला

questions.question: Choose the most suitable one-word substitution for the following. A person who pretends to have more knowledge than he actually possesses.
questions.explanation: B) Quack (noun) – A person who pretends to have knowledge or skill he does not actually possess झोलाछाप / ढोंगी • Cynic (noun) – A person who believes others are motivated by self-interest निंदक • Sage (noun) – A wise and knowledgeable person ज्ञानी • Mentor (noun) – An experienced guide or advisor मार्गदर्शक

questions.question: Choose the most suitable one-word substitution for the following. A person who studies animals.
questions.explanation: A) Zoologist (noun) – A person who studies animals, expert in zoology, animal scientist प्राणीशास्त्री • Botanist (noun) – A person who studies plants, plant scientist वनस्पतिशास्त्री • Anthropologist (noun) – A person who studies humans, human society and culture मानवशास्त्री • Pathologist (noun) – A person who studies diseases, medical scientist रोगविज्ञानी

questions.question: Select the option with the correct spelling that can replace the underlined word in the given sentence. If the spelling in the given sentence is already correct, choose Option “No replacement required”. The dissonent chords in the music created an unsettling and tense atmosphere.
questions.explanation: A) The correct spelling of ‘dissonent’ is ‘dissonant’, which means “lacking harmony; producing an unpleasant sound” असंगत, बेसुरा।

questions.question: Choose the correctly spelt word from the given options.
questions.explanation: C) The correct spelling of ‘Mischevous / Mischievos / Mischievus’ is ‘Mischievous’, which means “playfully causing trouble or harm” शरारती, नटखट।

questions.question: Which of the following options is the most suitable conversion of the following sentence into active voice? Which type of books are read by you?
options: Which type of books did you read?
Which type of books do you read?
Which type of books are you read?
Which type of books have you read?
questions.explanation: B) Which type of books do you read? Passive से Active Voice में बदलने के लिए, Passive वाक्य का Subject (agent) Active वाक्य का Subject बन जाता है और Passive का Subject Active का Object बन जाता है। Interrogative sentence में helping verb पहले आता है और tense वही रखा जाता है। दिए गए वाक्य में "Which type of books" Passive Subject है, "are read" Verb (Simple Present Passive) है और "by you" agent है। Active में बदलने पर "you" Subject बन गया है, Verb को "do read" (Simple Present Interrogative) में बदला गया है और "which type of books" Object के रूप में प्रयोग हुआ है। इसलिए सही वाक्य होगा — "Which type of books do you read?" To change a sentence from Passive to Active Voice, the agent becomes the subject and the passive subject becomes the object, while maintaining the same tense and interrogative structure. Here, "Which type of books" (Passive Subject), "are read" (Simple Present Passive), and "by you" (agent) are transformed into "you" (Subject), "do read" (Active Verb in Simple Present Interrogative), and "which type of books" (Object). Therefore, the correct sentence is — "Which type of books do you read?"

questions.question: Which of the following options is the most suitable conversion of the following sentence into active voice? The evidence was being carefully examined by the investigating officers at that time.
options: The investigating officers examined the evidence carefully at that time.
The investigating officers were carefully examining the evidence at that time.
The investigating officers have carefully examined the evidence at that time.
The investigating officers examine the evidence carefully at that time.
questions.explanation: B) The investigating officers were carefully examining the evidence at that time. Passive से Active Voice में बदलने के लिए, Passive वाक्य का Subject (agent) Active वाक्य का Subject बन जाता है और Passive का Subject Active का Object बन जाता है। Verb को Passive से Active में बदलते समय वही tense रखा जाता है। दिए गए वाक्य में "The evidence" Passive Subject है, "was being examined" Verb (Past Continuous Passive) है और "by the investigating officers" agent है। Active में बदलने पर "the investigating officers" Subject बन गया है, Verb को "were examining" (Past Continuous) में बदला गया है और "the evidence" Object के रूप में प्रयोग हुआ है। इसलिए सही वाक्य होगा — "The investigating officers were carefully examining the evidence at that time." To change a sentence from Passive to Active Voice, the agent becomes the subject and the passive subject becomes the object while keeping the same tense. Here, "The evidence" (Passive Subject), "was being examined" (Past Continuous Passive), and "by the investigating officers" (agent) are transformed into "the investigating officers" (Subject), "were examining" (Active Verb in Past Continuous), and "the evidence" (Object). Therefore, the correct sentence is — "The investigating officers were carefully examining the evidence at that time."

questions.question: Which of the following options is the most suitable conversion of the following sentence into Passive Voice? Why didn’t the police arrest the miscreants?
options: Why weren't the miscreants arrested by the police?
Why wasn't the miscreants arrested by the police?
Why didn't the miscreants arrested by the police?
Why don't the miscreants arrested by the police?
questions.explanation: A) Why weren't the miscreants arrested by the police? Active से Passive Voice में बदलने के लिए, Active वाक्य का Object Passive वाक्य का Subject बन जाता है। Negative interrogative sentence में helping verb और "not" का सही प्रयोग किया जाता है और tense वही रखा जाता है। दिए गए वाक्य में "the police" Subject है, "did not arrest" Verb (Simple Past Negative) है और "the miscreants" Object है। Passive में बदलने पर Object "the miscreants" Subject बन गया है, Verb को "were not arrested" (Simple Past Passive) में बदला गया है और "by the police" agent के रूप में प्रयोग हुआ है। इसलिए सही वाक्य होगा — "Why weren't the miscreants arrested by the police?" To change a sentence from Active to Passive Voice, the object becomes the subject, and in negative interrogative sentences, the helping verb and "not" are properly used while maintaining the same tense. Here, "the police" (Subject), "did not arrest" (Verb in Simple Past Negative), and "the miscreants" (Object) are transformed into "the miscreants" (Subject), "were not arrested" (Passive Verb in Simple Past), and "by the police" (agent). Therefore, the correct sentence is — "Why weren't the miscreants arrested by the police?"

questions.question: Choose the correct sentence where the homonym “letter” means a character of the alphabet.
options: She wrote a letter to her cousin in Canada.
The first letter of the word is capitalised.
He got a warning letter from his employer.
They followed the law to the letter.
questions.explanation: B) The first letter of the word is capitalised. अंग्रेजी में "letter" शब्द के दो मतलब होते हैं: एक होता है "पत्र" (जो हम डाक से भेजते हैं) और दूसरा होता है "अक्षर" (जैसे A, B, C)। प्रश्न में "alphabet" यानी "अक्षर" वाला मतलब पूछा गया है। विकल्प 2 में "capitalised" (बड़ा अक्षर बनाना) शब्द का इस्तेमाल हुआ है, जिससे पता चलता है कि यहाँ "letter" का मतलब वर्णमाला का अक्षर है। इसलिए सही वाक्य है — "The first letter of the word is capitalised." In simple terms, "letter" can mean a "mail" or a "symbol of the alphabet." The question asks for the "alphabet" meaning. Option 2 uses the word "capitalised," which refers to making an alphabet character big (Uppercase). This confirms that "letter" here means a character like A or B. Therefore, the correct sentence is — "The first letter of the word is capitalised."

questions.question: A part of the following sentence is underlined. Choose the option that can replace the underlined part to improve the sentence. In case Options A, B and C are not found suitable, choose Option D: “No Improvement Required.” The new policy was believing to have reduced corruption in the department.
questions.explanation: B) ‘was believing to have reduced’ के बदले ‘was believed to have reduced’ का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि believe का passive form believed होता है, न कि believing। • ‘was believed to have reduced’ will be used instead of ‘was believing to have reduced’ because in passive voice, the correct past form is “believed”, not “believing”.

questions.question: Improve the sentence by selecting the correct form of tense from the options. She said that she ______ the report before the deadline.
questions.explanation: C) ‘had completed’ का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि Indirect Speech में Reporting Verb ‘said’ (Simple Past) होने पर Reported Clause में tense एक step पीछे जाता है: Present Perfect ‘has completed’ → Past Perfect ‘had completed’; जैसे — He said that he had finished the work. • ‘had completed’ should be used because when the reporting verb is in the past tense (‘said’), the reported clause must use Past Perfect to indicate an action completed before another past action. • ‘will complete’ – Incorrect; future tense cannot be used after a past reporting verb in indirect speech. • ‘has completed’ – Incorrect; Present Perfect cannot be used after a past reporting verb; it must shift to Past Perfect. • ‘completes’ – Incorrect; Simple Present cannot follow a past reporting verb in indirect speech.

questions.question: Identify the part of the sentence that contains a grammatical error. A. The professors recommendation was B. that the student, C. whom she had worked with for years, D. be granted a full scholarship.
options: whom she had worked with for years
questions.explanation: A) इस sentence में grammatical error part A में है। यहाँ “professors” word plural form में लिखा गया है, जबकि sentence में possession दिखाना है, क्योंकि recommendation किसी एक professor की है। इसलिए सही form “professor’s” होगी, जिसमें apostrophe का use किया जाता है। बाकी sentence grammatically correct है। “whom” का use सही है क्योंकि यह object pronoun है, और “be granted” subjunctive form में सही तरीके से आया है, जो “recommendation” जैसे शब्दों के बाद use होता है। इसलिए error part 1 में है। The grammatical error occurs in part A of the sentence. The word “professors” is written as a plural noun, but in this context it is meant to show possession, because the recommendation belongs to one professor. Therefore, it should be written as “professor’s” with an apostrophe to indicate possession. The remaining parts of the sentence are grammatically correct: “whom” is properly used as an object pronoun, and “be granted” is correctly used in the subjunctive form after the noun “recommendation.” Hence, the error is in part 1.

questions.question: Identify the part of the sentence that contains a grammatical error. A. We need to B. discuss about the C. new marketing strategy D. in the next meeting.
questions.explanation: B) ‘discuss about’ के बदले केवल ‘discuss’ का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि ‘discuss’ के बाद preposition ‘about’ नहीं लगाया जाता; discuss स्वयं ‘about’ का अर्थ दे देता है। उदाहरण— We will discuss the plan tomorrow. • ‘discuss’ will be used instead of ‘discuss about’ because the verb discuss does not take the preposition about after it. The verb discuss itself means “talk about”, so adding about is grammatically incorrect. Example — We will discuss the plan tomorrow.

questions.question: Given below are six sentences taken from a paragraph. The first and the last sentences are numbered S1 and S2 and are placed in the first and last positions respectively and are fixed in their positions. The other four sentences between the first and last sentences are jumbled up and are numbered A, B, C, and D. Rearrange the jumbled sentences in a logical sequence keeping the unity and coherence of the paragraph intact. Choose the correct alternative that best describes the sequence. S1. At last Vladimir perceived that he was going in the wrong direction. A. His horse could scarcely move forward. B. He turned to the right now. C. He had now been on the road for more than an hour. D. He stopped, began to think, to recollect, and compare, and he felt convinced that he ought to have turned to the right. S2. Jadrino could not be far off.
questions.explanation: B) DBAC सबसे पहले Sentence D आएगा—S1 में उसे पता चलता है कि वह गलत दिशा में जा रहा है, इसलिए वह रुककर सोचता है—“he stopped, began to think…”. इसके बाद Sentence B आएगा क्योंकि अब वह निर्णय लेकर action करता है—“He turned to the right now.” फिर Sentence A आएगा क्योंकि यह उसकी स्थिति बताता है—“His horse could scarcely move forward.” अंत में Sentence C आएगा क्योंकि यह background देता है—“He had now been on the road for more than an hour,” और यह S2 से ठीक पहले logically fit बैठता है। Sentence D comes first—he stops and thinks after realizing he is going in the wrong direction (S1). Next, Sentence B follows because he acts on that realization—“He turned to the right now.” Then, Sentence A comes as it describes the condition of his horse—“His horse could scarcely move forward.” Finally, Sentence C comes last because it provides the background—“He had now been on the road for more than an hour,” which fits just before S2.

questions.question: Read the following passage carefully and answer the questions based on the passage. The concept of nihilism suggests that there is no inherent meaning, value, or truth in life. It has emerged in modern times, particularly as a result of Friedrich Nietzsche's ideas about the loss of tradition, a) religious or moral authority, and b) reason and logic, which were all once very strong in Western culture. Nihilism is mostly found in modern society, which has been experiencing rapid technological growth and an increasingly fragmented culture because of ideological fatigue and the pace of technological advancements. Nihilism produces feelings of isolation, skepticism, and apathy towards morality. While nihilism uncovers how fragile belief systems are, it also raises the question of how one can be held responsible, have a sense of purpose, or exercise free will without a basis in something like religious, metaphysical, or logical authority. Therefore, nihilism is not only a means of negating things but also an insightful way to highlight how each of us will have to deal with the emptiness created when something we once held to be true collapses, and to evaluate the basis of what we consider important. What is the main question that Nihilism asks about human existence?
options: How can someone find meaning when traditional sources of authority are gone?
Is having material wealth the only way to find happiness and fulfillment?
Should people reject all forms of morality and accept chaos instead?
Can technology by itself give people purpose and identity in today's world?
questions.explanation: A) How can someone find meaning when traditional sources of authority are gone?’ का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि पूरे संदर्भ में, passage यह बताता है कि nihilism पारंपरिक धार्मिक, नैतिक और तार्किक आधारों के समाप्त होने के बाद उत्पन्न शून्यता (emptiness) पर प्रश्न उठाता है। यहाँ यह मुख्य प्रश्न उठाया गया है कि जब जीवन में कोई निश्चित आधार नहीं बचता, तब व्यक्ति अपने उद्देश्य, जिम्मेदारी और अर्थ को कैसे स्थापित करे। इसलिए, विकल्प 1 सबसे उपयुक्त है। • ‘How can someone find meaning when traditional sources of authority are gone?’ should be used because the passage explains that nihilism questions the emptiness created after the collapse of traditional religious, moral, and logical foundations. It raises the central concern of how individuals can find purpose, responsibility, and meaning in the absence of such authority. Thus, option 1 is the most appropriate choice.

questions.question: Read the following passage carefully and answer the questions based on the passage. The concept of nihilism suggests that there is no inherent meaning, value, or truth in life. It has emerged in modern times, particularly as a result of Friedrich Nietzsche's ideas about the loss of tradition, a) religious or moral authority, and b) reason and logic, which were all once very strong in Western culture. Nihilism is mostly found in modern society, which has been experiencing rapid technological growth and an increasingly fragmented culture because of ideological fatigue and the pace of technological advancements. Nihilism produces feelings of isolation, skepticism, and apathy towards morality. While nihilism uncovers how fragile belief systems are, it also raises the question of how one can be held responsible, have a sense of purpose, or exercise free will without a basis in something like religious, metaphysical, or logical authority. Therefore, nihilism is not only a means of negating things but also an insightful way to highlight how each of us will have to deal with the emptiness created when something we once held to be true collapses, and to evaluate the basis of what we consider important. What does the concept of Nihilism primarily suggest?
options: Life is guided by eternal moral principles
Life has no inherent meaning, value, or truth
Religion is the only source of meaning in life
questions.explanation: B) ‘Life has no inherent meaning, value, or truth’ का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि passage की पहली ही पंक्ति में nihilism की परिभाषा दी गई है कि जीवन में कोई अंतर्निहित अर्थ, मूल्य या सत्य नहीं होता। यह पूरे संदर्भ का मुख्य विचार है। इसलिए, विकल्प 2 सबसे उपयुक्त है। • ‘Life has no inherent meaning, value, or truth’ should be used because the very first line of the passage defines nihilism as the belief that life lacks inherent meaning, value, or truth. This is the central idea conveyed throughout the passage. Thus, option 2 is the most appropriate choice.

questions.question: Read the following passage carefully and answer the questions based on the passage. The concept of nihilism suggests that there is no inherent meaning, value, or truth in life. It has emerged in modern times, particularly as a result of Friedrich Nietzsche's ideas about the loss of tradition, a) religious or moral authority, and b) reason and logic, which were all once very strong in Western culture. Nihilism is mostly found in modern society, which has been experiencing rapid technological growth and an increasingly fragmented culture because of ideological fatigue and the pace of technological advancements. Nihilism produces feelings of isolation, skepticism, and apathy towards morality. While nihilism uncovers how fragile belief systems are, it also raises the question of how one can be held responsible, have a sense of purpose, or exercise free will without a basis in something like religious, metaphysical, or logical authority. Therefore, nihilism is not only a means of negating things but also an insightful way to highlight how each of us will have to deal with the emptiness created when something we once held to be true collapses, and to evaluate the basis of what we consider important. What modern conditions have contributed to Nihilism's spread?
options: The increased level of global integration among societies that are still heavily dedicated to their Established Values
The almost universal application of Religious and Moral Authority by a good many members of modern society
The Rapid growth in technology and a fragmented culture resulting from ideological fatigue
The Resurgence in the place of Logic and Metaphysics within the community
questions.explanation: C) The Rapid growth in technology and a fragmented culture resulting from ideological fatigue’ का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि passage में स्पष्ट रूप से बताया गया है कि modern society में तेज़ technological growth और ideological fatigue के कारण culture fragmented हो गया है, जिससे nihilism का प्रसार हुआ है। इसलिए, विकल्प 3 सबसे उपयुक्त है। • ‘The Rapid growth in technology and a fragmented culture resulting from ideological fatigue’ should be used because the passage clearly states that nihilism has spread in modern society due to rapid technological advancements and a fragmented culture caused by ideological fatigue. Thus, option 3 is the most appropriate choice.

questions.question: Read the following passage carefully and answer the questions based on the passage. The concept of nihilism suggests that there is no inherent meaning, value, or truth in life. It has emerged in modern times, particularly as a result of Friedrich Nietzsche's ideas about the loss of tradition, a) religious or moral authority, and b) reason and logic, which were all once very strong in Western culture. Nihilism is mostly found in modern society, which has been experiencing rapid technological growth and an increasingly fragmented culture because of ideological fatigue and the pace of technological advancements. Nihilism produces feelings of isolation, skepticism, and apathy towards morality. While nihilism uncovers how fragile belief systems are, it also raises the question of how one can be held responsible, have a sense of purpose, or exercise free will without a basis in something like religious, metaphysical, or logical authority. Therefore, nihilism is not only a means of negating things but also an insightful way to highlight how each of us will have to deal with the emptiness created when something we once held to be true collapses, and to evaluate the basis of what we consider important. Why is Nihilism considered more than mere negation?
options: By denying all systems of meaning altogether, it fails to provide any kind of interpretation or analysis of life
By rejecting all systems of meaning, it encourages blind acceptance of materialism and consumerism
By insisting on strict moral guidelines to fill our lives with meaning, it is trying to fill the void of existential emptiness
By demonstrating how fragile each system of belief is, it prompts us evaluate what truly matters in life
questions.explanation: D) By demonstrating how fragile each system of belief is, it prompts us evaluate what truly matters in life’ का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि passage में बताया गया है कि nihilism केवल नकार (negation) नहीं है, बल्कि यह belief systems की नाजुकता को उजागर करता है और हमें यह सोचने पर मजबूर करता है कि वास्तव में जीवन में क्या महत्वपूर्ण है। इसलिए, विकल्प 4 सबसे उपयुक्त है। • ‘By demonstrating how fragile each system of belief is, it prompts us evaluate what truly matters in life’ should be used because the passage explains that nihilism is not just about negation; it reveals the fragility of belief systems and encourages us to reassess what truly holds importance in life. Thus, option 4 is the most appropriate choice.

questions.question: Read the following passage carefully and answer the questions based on the passage. The concept of nihilism suggests that there is no inherent meaning, value, or truth in life. It has emerged in modern times, particularly as a result of Friedrich Nietzsche's ideas about the loss of tradition, a) religious or moral authority, and b) reason and logic, which were all once very strong in Western culture. Nihilism is mostly found in modern society, which has been experiencing rapid technological growth and an increasingly fragmented culture because of ideological fatigue and the pace of technological advancements. Nihilism produces feelings of isolation, skepticism, and apathy towards morality. While nihilism uncovers how fragile belief systems are, it also raises the question of how one can be held responsible, have a sense of purpose, or exercise free will without a basis in something like religious, metaphysical, or logical authority. Therefore, nihilism is not only a means of negating things but also an insightful way to highlight how each of us will have to deal with the emptiness created when something we once held to be true collapses, and to evaluate the basis of what we consider important. How does Nihilism usually affect people emotionally?
options: It can lead to feelings of isolation, skepticism, and not caring much about morality.
It can give a sense of empowerment and a clear idea about life's ultimate purpose.
It can make people feel more optimistic and confident in universal truths.
Stronger commitment to religious and ethical traditions.
questions.explanation: A) It can lead to feelings of isolation, skepticism, and not caring much about morality.’ का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि passage में स्पष्ट रूप से बताया गया है कि nihilism लोगों में isolation, skepticism और morality के प्रति उदासीनता (apathy) जैसी भावनाएँ उत्पन्न करता है। इसलिए, विकल्प 1 सबसे उपयुक्त है। • ‘It can lead to feelings of isolation, skepticism, and not caring much about morality.’ should be used because the passage clearly states that nihilism produces feelings of isolation, skepticism, and apathy toward morality. Thus, option 1 is the most appropriate choice.
